v.CNV: 安提阿教会中，有几位先知和教师，就是巴拿巴、名叫尼结的西面、古利奈人路求、与分封王希律一同长大的马念，和扫罗。
v.ESV: Now there were in the church at Antioch prophets and teachers, Barnabas, Simeon who was called Niger, Lucius of Cyrene, Manaen a lifelong friend of Herod the tetrarch, and Saul.
v.GAE: 안디옥 교회에 선지자들과 교사들이 있으니 곧 바나바와 니게르라 하는 시므온과 구레네 사람 루기오와 분봉 왕 헤롯의 젖동생 마나엔과 및 사울이라
v.KJV: Now there were in the church that was at Antioch certain prophets and teachers; as Barnabas, and Simeon that was called Niger, and Lucius of Cyrene, and Manaen, which had been brought up with Herod the tetrarch, and Saul.
v.NASB: Now there were at Antioch, in the church that was there, prophets and teachers: Barnabas, and Simeon who was called Niger, and Lucius of Cyrene, and Manaen who had been brought up with Herod the tetrarch, and Saul.
v.NIV: Now in the church at Antioch there were prophets and teachers: Barnabas, Simeon called Niger, Lucius of Cyrene, Manaen (who had been brought up with Herod the tetrarch) and Saul.
v.NRSV: Now in the church at Antioch there were prophets and teachers: Barnabas, Simeon who was called Niger, Lucius of Cyrene, Manaen a member of the court of Herod the ruler, and Saul.
v.SAENEW: 안디옥 교회에 예언자들과 교사들이 있었는데, 그들은 바나바와 니게르라고 하는 시므온과, 구레네 사람 루기오와 분봉왕 헤롯과 더불어 어릴 때부터 함께 자란 마나엔과 사울이다.
v.WLB: 안디옥 교회에는 예언자들과 교사들이 있었습니다. 그들은 바나바, 니게르라고 하는 시므온, 구레네 사람 루기오, 분봉왕 헤롯 안티파스와 어릴 때부터 함께 자란 마나엔 그리고 사울이었습니다.

v.CNV: 他们事奉主，并且禁食的时候，圣灵说：“要为我把巴拿巴和扫罗分别出来，去作我呼召他们作的工。”
v.ESV: While they were worshiping the Lord and fasting, the Holy Spirit said, “Set apart for me Barnabas and Saul for the work to which I have called them.”
v.GAE: 주를 섬겨 금식할 때에 성령이 이르시되 내가 불러 시키는 일을 위하여 바나바와 사울을 따로 세우라 하시니
v.KJV: As they ministered to the Lord, and fasted, the Holy Ghost said, Separate me Barnabas and Saul for the work whereunto I have called them.
v.NASB: While they were ministering to the Lord and fasting, the Holy Spirit said, 'Set apart for Me Barnabas and Saul for the work to which I have called them.'
v.NIV: While they were worshiping the Lord and fasting, the Holy Spirit said, “Set apart for me Barnabas and Saul for the work to which I have called them.”
v.NRSV: While they were worshiping the Lord and fasting, the Holy Spirit said, “Set apart for me Barnabas and Saul for the work to which I have called them.”
v.SAENEW: 그들이 주님께 예배하며 금식하고 있을 때에, 성령이 그들에게 말씀하셨다. "너희는 나를 위해서 바나바와 사울을 따로 세워라. 내가 그들에게 맡기려 하는 일이 있다."
v.WLB: 그들이 주께 예배드리며 금식하고 있을 때 성령께서 그들에게 “너희는 바나바와 사울을 따로 세워 내가 그들에게 맡긴 일을 하게 하라”고 말씀하셨습니다.

v.CNV: 于是他们禁食祷告，为两人按手，就派他们去了。
v.ESV: Then after fasting and praying they laid their hands on them and sent them off.
v.GAE: 이에 금식하며 기도하고 두 사람에게 안수하여 보내니라
v.KJV: And when they had fasted and prayed, and laid their hands on them, they sent them away.
v.NASB: Then, when they had fasted and prayed and laid their hands on them, they sent them away.
v.NIV: So after they had fasted and prayed, they placed their hands on them and sent them off.
v.NRSV: Then after fasting and praying they laid their hands on them and sent them off.
v.SAENEW: 그래서 그들은 금식하고 기도한 뒤에, 두 사람에게 안수를 하여 떠나보냈다.
v.WLB: 그래서 그들은 금식하며 기도한 후 바나바와 사울에게 손을 얹고 그들을 보냈습니다.

v.CNV: 他们既然奉圣灵差遣，就下到西流基，从那里坐船往塞浦路斯。
v.ESV: So, being sent out by the Holy Spirit, they went down to Seleucia, and from there they sailed to Cyprus.
v.GAE: 두 사람이 성령의 보내심을 받아 실루기아에 내려가 거기서 배 타고 구브로에 가서
v.KJV: So they, being sent forth by the Holy Ghost, departed unto Seleucia; and from thence they sailed to Cyprus.
v.NASB: So, being sent out by the Holy Spirit, they went down to Seleucia and from there they sailed to Cyprus.
v.NIV: The two of them, sent on their way by the Holy Spirit, went down to Seleucia and sailed from there to Cyprus.
v.NRSV: So, being sent out by the Holy Spirit, they went down to Seleucia; and from there they sailed to Cyprus.
v.SAENEW: 바나바와 사울은, 성령이 가라고 보내시므로, 실루기아로 내려가서, 거기에서 배를 타고 키프로스로 건너갔다.
v.WLB: 그 두 사람은 성령의 보내심을 받아 실루기아로 내려갔다가 거기서 배를 타고 키프로스로 가게 됐습니다.

v.CNV: 他们到了撒拉米，就在犹太人的各会堂里宣讲　神的道，还有约翰作他们的助手。
v.ESV: When they arrived at Salamis, they proclaimed the word of God in the synagogues of the Jews. And they had John to assist them.
v.GAE: 살라미에 이르러 하나님의 말씀을 유대인의 여러 회당에서 전할새 요한을 수행원으로 두었더라
v.KJV: And when they were at Salamis, they preached the word of God in the synagogues of the Jews: and they had also John to their minister.
v.NASB: When they reached Salamis, they began to proclaim the word of God in the synagogues of the Jews; and they also had John as their helper.
v.NIV: When they arrived at Salamis, they proclaimed the word of God in the Jewish synagogues. John was with them as their helper.
v.NRSV: When they arrived at Salamis, they proclaimed the word of God in the synagogues of the Jews. And they had John also to assist them.
v.SAENEW: 그들은 살라미에 이르러서, 유대 사람의 여러 회당에서 하나님의 말씀을 전하였다. 그들은 요한도 또한 조수로 데리고 있었다.
v.WLB: 그들이 살라미에 이르러서는 유대 사람의 여러 회당에서 하나님의 말씀을 선포했습니다. 요한이 동행하며 그들을 도왔습니다.

v.CNV: 他们走遍全岛，到了帕弗，遇见一个犹太人，名叫巴．耶稣，是个术士，又是个假先知。
v.ESV: When they had gone through the whole island as far as Paphos, they came upon a certain magician, a Jewish false prophet named Bar-Jesus.
v.GAE: 온 섬 가운데로 지나서 바보에 이르러 바예수라 하는 유대인 거짓 선지자인 마술사를 만나니
v.KJV: And when they had gone through the isle unto Paphos, they found a certain sorcerer, a false prophet, a Jew, whose name was Bar-jesus:
v.NASB: When they had gone through the whole island as far as Paphos, they found a magician, a Jewish false prophet whose name was Bar-Jesus,
v.NIV: They traveled through the whole island until they came to Paphos. There they met a Jewish sorcerer and false prophet named Bar-Jesus,
v.NRSV: When they had gone through the whole island as far as Paphos, they met a certain magician, a Jewish false prophet, named Bar-Jesus.
v.SAENEW: 그들은 온 섬을 가로질러 바보에 이르렀다. 거기서 그들은 어떤 마술사를 만났는데, 그는 거짓 예언자였으며 바예수라고 하는 유대인이었다.
v.WLB: 그들이 그 섬을 가로질러 바보에 도착했습니다. 거기서 그들은 바예수라는 사람을 만났는데 그는 유대 사람 마술사요, 거짓 예언자로서

v.CNV: 他常常和省长士求．保罗在一起；省长是个聪明人，他请了巴拿巴和扫罗来，要听听　神的道。
v.ESV: He was with the proconsul, Sergius Paulus, a man of intelligence, who summoned Barnabas and Saul and sought to hear the word of God.
v.GAE: 그가 총독 서기오 바울과 함께 있으니 서기오 바울은 지혜 있는 사람이라 바나바와 사울을 불러 하나님의 말씀을 듣고자 하더라
v.KJV: Which was with the deputy of the country, Sergius Paulus, a prudent man; who called for Barnabas and Saul, and desired to hear the word of God.
v.NASB: who was with the proconsul, Sergius Paulus, a man of intelligence. This man summoned Barnabas and Saul and sought to hear the word of God.
v.NIV: who was an attendant of the proconsul, Sergius Paulus. The proconsul, an intelligent man, sent for Barnabas and Saul because he wanted to hear the word of God.
v.NRSV: He was with the proconsul, Sergius Paulus, an intelligent man, who summoned Barnabas and Saul and wanted to hear the word of God.
v.SAENEW: 그는 총독 서기오 바울을 늘 곁에서 모시는 사람이었다. 이 총독은 총명한 사람이어서, 바나바와 사울을 청해서, 하나님의 말씀을 듣고자 하였다.
v.WLB: 총독 서기오 바울 곁에 함께 있는 사람이었습니다. 지성적이었던 총독은 하나님의 말씀을 듣고 싶어 바나바와 사울에게 사람을 보냈습니다.

v.CNV: 但术士以吕马（以吕马就是“术士”的意思）与使徒作对，要使省长转离真道。
v.ESV: But Elymas the magician (for that is the meaning of his name) opposed them, seeking to turn the proconsul away from the faith.
v.GAE: 이 마술사 엘루마는 (이 이름을 번역하면 마술사라) 그들을 대적하여 총독으로 믿지 못하게 힘쓰니
v.KJV: But Elymas the sorcerer (for so is his name by interpretation) withstood them, seeking to turn away the deputy from the faith.
v.NASB: But Elymas the magician (for so his name is translated) was opposing them, seeking to turn the proconsul away from the faith.
v.NIV: But Elymas the sorcerer (for that is what his name means) opposed them and tried to turn the proconsul from the faith.
v.NRSV: But the magician Elymas (for that is the translation of his name) opposed them and tried to turn the proconsul away from the faith.
v.SAENEW: 그런데 이름을 엘루마라고 번역해서 부르기도 하는 그 마술사가 그들을 방해하여, 총독으로 하여금 믿지 못하게 하려고 애를 썼다.
v.WLB: 그러나 엘루마라고도 하는 이 마술사가 그들을 막으며 총독이 믿음을 갖지 못하게 하려고 애를 썼습니다.

v.CNV: 扫罗，也就是保罗，却被圣灵充满，定睛看着他，
v.ESV: But Saul, who was also called Paul, filled with the Holy Spirit, looked intently at him
v.GAE: 바울이라고 하는 사울이 성령이 충만하여 그를 주목하고
v.KJV: Then Saul, (who also is called Paul,) filled with the Holy Ghost, set his eyes on him,
v.NASB: But Saul, who was also known as Paul, filled with the Holy Spirit, fixed his gaze on him,
v.NIV: Then Saul, who was also called Paul, filled with the Holy Spirit, looked straight at Elymas and said,
v.NRSV: But Saul, also known as Paul, filled with the Holy Spirit, looked intently at him
v.SAENEW: 그래서 바울이라고도 하는 사울이 성령으로 충만하여 마술사를 노려보고 말하였다.
v.WLB: 그러자 바울이라고도 하는 사울이 성령충만한 가운데 엘루마를 뚫어지게 쳐다보며 말했습니다.

v.CNV: 说：“你这充满各样诡诈和各样奸恶的人，魔鬼的儿子，公义的仇敌！你歪曲了主的正路，还不停止吗？
v.ESV: and said, “You son of the devil, you enemy of all righteousness, full of all deceit and villainy, will you not stop making crooked the straight paths of the Lord?
v.GAE: 이르되 모든 거짓과 악행이 가득한 자요 마귀의 자식이요 모든 의의 원수여 주의 바른 길을 굽게 하기를 그치지 아니하겠느냐
v.KJV: And said, O full of all subtilty and all mischief, thou child of the devil, thou enemy of all righteousness, wilt thou not cease to pervert the right ways of the Lord?
v.NASB: and said, 'You who are full of all deceit and fraud, you son of the devil, you enemy of all righteousness, will you not cease to make crooked the straight ways of the Lord?
v.NIV: “You are a child of the devil and an enemy of everything that is right! You are full of all kinds of deceit and trickery. Will you never stop perverting the right ways of the Lord?
v.NRSV: and said, “You son of the devil, you enemy of all righteousness, full of all deceit and villainy, will you not stop making crooked the straight paths of the Lord?
v.SAENEW: "너, 속임수와 악행으로 가득 찬 악마의 자식아, 모든 정의의 원수야, 너는 주님의 바른 길을 굽게 하는 짓을 그치지 못하겠느냐?
v.WLB: “이 마귀의 자식아! 너는 모든 의의 원수로다! 너는 갖은 속임수와 거짓으로 가득 차 있구나. 주의 바른 길을 어지럽게 하는 일을 그치지 못하겠느냐?

v.CNV: 你看，现在主的手临到你，你要瞎了眼睛，暂时看不见阳光。”他就立刻被雾和黑暗笼罩着，周围找人牵他的手，给他领路。
v.ESV: And now, behold, the hand of the Lord is upon you, and you will be blind and unable to see the sun for a time.” Immediately mist and darkness fell upon him, and he went about seeking people to lead him by the hand.
v.GAE: 보라 이제 주의 손이 네 위에 있으니 네가 맹인이 되어 얼마 동안 해를 보지 못하리라 하니 즉시 안개와 어둠이 그를 덮어 인도할 사람을 두루 구하는지라
v.KJV: And now, behold, the hand of the Lord is upon thee, and thou shalt be blind, not seeing the sun for a season. And immediately there fell on him a mist and a darkness; and he went about seeking some to lead him by the hand.
v.NASB: 'Now, behold, the hand of the Lord is upon you, and you will be blind and not see the sun for a time.' And immediately a mist and a darkness fell upon him, and he went about seeking those who would lead him by the hand.
v.NIV: Now the hand of the Lord is against you. You are going to be blind for a time, not even able to see the light of the sun.” Immediately mist and darkness came over him, and he groped about, seeking someone to lead him by the hand.
v.NRSV: And now listen — the hand of the Lord is against you, and you will be blind for a while, unable to see the sun.” Immediately mist and darkness came over him, and he went about groping for someone to lead him by the hand.
v.SAENEW: 보아라, 이제 주님의 손이 너를 내리칠 것이니, 눈이 멀어서 얼마 동안 햇빛을 보지 못할 것이다." 그러자 곧 안개와 어둠이 그를 내리덮어서, 그는 앞을 더듬으면서, 손을 잡아 자기를 이끌어 줄 사람을 찾았다.
v.WLB: 이제 주의 손이 너를 치심으로 네 눈이 멀어 얼마 동안 햇빛을 보지 못할 것이다.” 그러자 즉시 안개와 어둠이 그를 덮쳤고 그는 이리저리 더듬으며 자기 손을 잡아 이끌어 줄 사람을 찾았습니다.

v.CNV: 那时，省长看见了所发生的事，就信了，因为他惊奇主的教训。
v.ESV: Then the proconsul believed, when he saw what had occurred, for he was astonished at the teaching of the Lord.
v.GAE: 이에 총독이 그렇게 된 것을 보고 믿으며 주의 가르치심을 놀랍게 여기니라
v.KJV: Then the deputy, when he saw what was done, believed, being astonished at the doctrine of the Lord.
v.NASB: Then the proconsul believed when he saw what had happened, being amazed at the teaching of the Lord.
v.NIV: When the proconsul saw what had happened, he believed, for he was amazed at the teaching about the Lord.
v.NRSV: When the proconsul saw what had happened, he believed, for he was astonished at the teaching about the Lord.
v.SAENEW: 총독은 그 일어난 일을 보고 주님을 믿게 되었고, 주님의 교훈에 깊은 감명을 받았다.
v.WLB: 이 일을 보자 총독이 믿게 됐고 주에 관한 가르침에 그저 놀랄 뿐이었습니다.

v.CNV: 保罗和同伴从帕弗开船，来到旁非利亚的别加，约翰却离开他们，回耶路撒冷去了。
v.ESV: Now Paul and his companions set sail from Paphos and came to Perga in Pamphylia. And John left them and returned to Jerusalem,
v.GAE: 바울과 및 동행하는 사람들이 바보에서 배 타고 밤빌리아에 있는 버가에 이르니 요한은 그들에게서 떠나 예루살렘으로 돌아가고
v.KJV: Now when Paul and his company loosed from Paphos, they came to Perga in Pamphylia: and John departing from them returned to Jerusalem.
v.NASB: Now Paul and his companions put out to sea from Paphos and came to Perga in Pamphylia; but John left them and returned to Jerusalem.
v.NIV: From Paphos, Paul and his companions sailed to Perga in Pamphylia, where John left them to return to Jerusalem.
v.NRSV: Then Paul and his companions set sail from Paphos and came to Perga in Pamphylia. John, however, left them and returned to Jerusalem;
v.SAENEW: 바울과 그 일행은 바보에서 배를 타고, 밤빌리아에 있는 버가로 건너갔다. 그런데 요한은 그들과 헤어져서 예루살렘으로 돌아갔다.
v.WLB: 바울과 그 일행은 바보에서 배를 타고 밤빌리아에 있는 버가에 이르렀습니다. 그곳에서 요한은 그들과 헤어져 예루살렘으로 돌아갔습니다.

v.CNV: 他们从别加往前走，到了彼西底的安提阿，在安息日进了会堂，就坐下来。
v.ESV: but they went on from Perga and came to Antioch in Pisidia. And on the Sabbath day they went into the synagogue and sat down.
v.GAE: 그들은 버가에서 더 나아가 비시디아 안디옥에 이르러 안식일에 회당에 들어가 앉으니라
v.KJV: But when they departed from Perga, they came to Antioch in Pisidia, and went into the synagogue on the sabbath day, and sat down.
v.NASB: But going on from Perga, they arrived at Pisidian Antioch, and on the Sabbath day they went into the synagogue and sat down.
v.NIV: From Perga they went on to Pisidian Antioch. On the Sabbath they entered the synagogue and sat down.
v.NRSV: but they went on from Perga and came to Antioch in Pisidia. And on the sabbath day they went into the synagogue and sat down.
v.SAENEW: 그들은 버가에서 더 나아가, 비시디아의 안디옥에 이르러서, 안식일에 회당에 들어가 앉았다.
v.WLB: 그들은 버가에서 더 나아가 비시디아 안디옥으로 갔습니다. 그리고 안식일이 돼 회당에 들어가 앉았습니다.

v.CNV: 宣读了律法和先知书以后，会堂的理事们派人到他们那里来，说：“弟兄们，如果有什么劝勉众人的话，请说吧！”
v.ESV: After the reading from the Law and the Prophets, the rulers of the synagogue sent a message to them, saying, “Brothers, if you have any word of encouragement for the people, say it.”
v.GAE: 율법과 선지자의 글을 읽은 후에 회당장들이 사람을 보내어 물어 이르되 형제들아 만일 백성을 권할 말이 있거든 말하라 하니
v.KJV: And after the reading of the law and the prophets the rulers of the synagogue sent unto them, saying, Ye men and brethren, if ye have any word of exhortation for the people, say on.
v.NASB: After the reading of the Law and the Prophets the synagogue officials sent to them, saying, 'Brethren, if you have any word of exhortation for the people, say it.'
v.NIV: After the reading from the Law and the Prophets, the leaders of the synagogue sent word to them, saying, “Brothers, if you have a word of exhortation for the people, please speak.”
v.NRSV: After the reading of the law and the prophets, the officials of the synagogue sent them a message, saying, “Brothers, if you have any word of exhortation for the people, give it.”
v.SAENEW: 율법서와 예언자의 글을 낭독한 뒤에, 회당장들이 바울과 바나바에게 사람을 보내어 "형제들이여, 이 사람들에게 권면할 말씀이 있으면 해주시오" 하고 청하였다.
v.WLB: 율법서와 예언자들의 글을 낭독한 뒤에 회당 지도자들이 바울과 바나바에게 전갈을 보내 “형제들이여, 이 사람들에게 권면해 줄 말씀이 있으면 하시오”라고 했습니다.

v.CNV: 保罗就站起来，作了一个手势，说：“以色列人和敬畏　神的人，请听！
v.ESV: So Paul stood up, and motioning with his hand said: “Men of Israel and you who fear God, listen.
v.GAE: 바울이 일어나 손짓하며 말하되 이스라엘 사람들과 및 하나님을 경외하는 사람들아 들으라
v.KJV: Then Paul stood up, and beckoning with his hand said, Men of Israel, and ye that fear God, give audience.
v.NASB: Paul stood up, and motioning with his hand said, 'Men of Israel, and you who fear God, listen:
v.NIV: Standing up, Paul motioned with his hand and said: “Fellow Israelites and you Gentiles who worship God, listen to me!
v.NRSV: So Paul stood up and with a gesture began to speak: “You Israelites, and others who fear God, listen.
v.SAENEW: 그래서 바울은 일어나서, 손을 흔들고 말하였다."이스라엘 동포 여러분, 그리고 하나님을 두려워하는 사람들이여, 내 말을 들으십시오.
v.WLB: 바울이 일어나 손짓하며 말했습니다. “이스라엘 사람들 또 하나님을 경외하는 이방 사람들이여, 내 말을 들으십시오.

v.CNV: 以色列民的　神，拣选了我们的祖先；当他们在埃及地寄居的时候，　神抬举这民，用大能（“大能”原文作“高”）的膀臂，把他们从那地领出来；
v.ESV: The God of this people Israel chose our fathers and made the people great during their stay in the land of Egypt, and with uplifted arm he led them out of it.
v.GAE: 이 이스라엘 백성의 하나님이 우리 조상들을 택하시고 애굽 땅에서 나그네 된 그 백성을 높여 큰 권능으로 인도하여 내사
v.KJV: The God of this people of Israel chose our fathers, and exalted the people when they dwelt as strangers in the land of Egypt, and with an high arm brought he them out of it.
v.NASB: 'The God of this people Israel chose our fathers and made the people great during their stay in the land of Egypt, and with an uplifted arm He led them out from it.
v.NIV: The God of the people of Israel chose our ancestors; he made the people prosper during their stay in Egypt; with mighty power he led them out of that country;
v.NRSV: The God of this people Israel chose our ancestors and made the people great during their stay in the land of Egypt, and with uplifted arm he led them out of it.
v.SAENEW: 이 백성 이스라엘의 하나님께서 우리 조상들을 택하셨습니다. 이 백성이 이집트 땅에서 나그네 생활을 하는 동안에, 이 백성을 높여 주시고, 권능의 팔로 그들을 거기에서 인도하여 내셨습니다.
v.WLB: 이스라엘 백성의 하나님께서는 우리 조상들을 선택하셔서 이 민족이 이집트 땅에 사는 동안 큰 민족이 되게 하시고 큰 권능으로 그들을 그 나라에서 이끌어 내셨습니다.

v.CNV: 又在旷野容忍（“容忍”有些抄本作“养育”）他们，约有四十年之久；
v.ESV: And for about forty years he put up with them in the wilderness.
v.GAE: 광야에서 약 사십 년간 그들의 소행을 참으시고
v.KJV: And about the time of forty years suffered he their manners in the wilderness.
v.NASB: 'For a period of about forty years He put up with them in the wilderness.
v.NIV: for about forty years he endured their conduct in the wilderness;
v.NRSV: For about forty years he put up with them in the wilderness.
v.SAENEW: 광야에서는 사십 년 동안 그들에 대하여 참아 주시고,
v.WLB: 광야에서 40년 동안 그들의 소행을 참으시고

v.CNV: 灭了迦南地的七族之后，就把那地分给他们为业；
v.ESV: And after destroying seven nations in the land of Canaan, he gave them their land as an inheritance.
v.GAE: 가나안 땅 일곱 족속을 멸하사 그 땅을 기업으로 주시기까지 약 사백오십 년간이라
v.KJV: And when he had destroyed seven nations in the land of Chanaan, he divided their land to them by lot.
v.NASB: 'When He had destroyed seven nations in the land of Canaan, He distributed their land as an inheritance-- all of which took about four hundred and fifty years.
v.NIV: and he overthrew seven nations in Canaan, giving their land to his people as their inheritance.
v.NRSV: After he had destroyed seven nations in the land of Canaan, he gave them their land as an inheritance
v.SAENEW: 가나안 땅의 일곱 족속을 멸하셔서, 그 땅을 그들에게 유업으로 주시고,
v.WLB: 가나안 땅의 일곱 민족을 멸하셔서 그 땅을 이스라엘 백성들에게 유업으로 주셨습니다.

v.CNV: 这一切历时约四百五十年。后来　神赐给他们士师，直到撒母耳先知为止。
v.ESV: All this took about 450 years. And after that he gave them judges until Samuel the prophet.
v.GAE: 그 후에 선지자 사무엘 때까지 사사를 주셨더니
v.KJV: And after that he gave unto them judges about the space of four hundred and fifty years, until Samuel the prophet.
v.NASB: 'After these things He gave them judges until Samuel the prophet.
v.NIV: All this took about 450 years. “After this, God gave them judges until the time of Samuel the prophet.
v.NRSV: for about four hundred fifty years. After that he gave them judges until the time of the prophet Samuel.
v.SAENEW: 약 사백오십 년 동안 차지하게 하셨습니다. 그 뒤에 예언자 사무엘 시대에 이르기까지는 사사들을 보내주시고,
v.WLB: 이 모든 일은 450여 년에 걸쳐 일어났습니다. 그 뒤 하나님께서는 그들에게 예언자 사무엘 시대까지 사사들을 세워 주셨습니다.

v.CNV: 那时，他们要求立一个王，　神就把便雅悯支派中一个人，基士的儿子扫罗，赐给他们作王，共四十年之久。
v.ESV: Then they asked for a king, and God gave them Saul the son of Kish, a man of the tribe of Benjamin, for forty years.
v.GAE: 그 후에 그들이 왕을 구하거늘 하나님이 베냐민 지파 사람 기스의 아들 사울을 사십 년간 주셨다가
v.KJV: And afterward they desired a king: and God gave unto them Saul the son of Cis, a man of the tribe of Benjamin, by the space of forty years.
v.NASB: 'Then they asked for a king, and God gave them Saul the son of Kish, a man of the tribe of Benjamin, for forty years.
v.NIV: Then the people asked for a king, and he gave them Saul son of Kish, of the tribe of Benjamin, who ruled forty years.
v.NRSV: Then they asked for a king; and God gave them Saul son of Kish, a man of the tribe of Benjamin, who reigned for forty years.
v.SAENEW: 그 뒤에 그들이 왕을 요구하기에, 하나님께서는 베냐민 지파 사람 기스의 아들 사울을 그들에게 왕으로 주셔서, 사십 년 동안 그를 왕으로 섬기게 하셨습니다.
v.WLB: 그 후 이 백성들이 왕을 요구하자 하나님께서 베냐민 지파 기스의 아들 사울을 주어 40년 동안 다스리게 하셨습니다.

v.CNV: 废去扫罗之后，又为他们兴起大卫作王，并且为他作证说：‘我找到耶西的儿子大卫，他是合我心意的人，必遵行我的一切旨意。’
v.ESV: And when he had removed him, he raised up David to be their king, of whom he testified and said, ‘I have found in David the son of Jesse a man after my heart, who will do all my will.’
v.GAE: 폐하시고 다윗을 왕으로 세우시고 증언하여 이르시되 내가 이새의 아들 다윗을 만나니 내 마음에 맞는 사람이라 내 뜻을 다 이루리라 하시더니
v.KJV: And when he had removed him, he raised up unto them David to be their king; to whom also he gave testimony, and said, I have found David the son of Jesse, a man after mine own heart, which shall fulfil all my will.
v.NASB: 'After He had removed him, He raised up David to be their king, concerning whom He also testified and said, 'I HAVE FOUND DAVID the son of Jesse, A MAN AFTER MY HEART, who will do all My will.'
v.NIV: After removing Saul, he made David their king. God testified concerning him: ‘I have found David son of Jesse, a man after my own heart; he will do everything I want him to do.’
v.NRSV: When he had removed him, he made David their king. In his testimony about him he said, ‘I have found David, son of Jesse, to be a man after my heart, who will carry out all my wishes.’
v.SAENEW: 그 다음에 하나님께서는 사울을 물리치시고서, 다윗을 그들의 왕으로 세우시고, 증언하여 말씀하시기를 '내가 이새의 아들 다윗을 찾아냈으니, 그는 내 마음에 드는 사람이다. 그가 내 뜻을 다 행할 것이다' 하셨습니다.
v.WLB: 그리고 사울을 폐하신 하나님께서는 다윗을 그들의 왕으로 세우셨습니다. 다윗에 대해 증언하시기를 ‘내가 이새의 아들 다윗을 보니 내 마음에 꼭 맞는 사람임을 알았다. 내가 바라는 모든 것을 그가 이룰 것이다’라고 하셨습니다.

v.CNV: 神照着应许，已经从这人的后裔中，给以色列带来了一位救主，就是耶稣。
v.ESV: Of this man's offspring God has brought to Israel a Savior, Jesus, as he promised.
v.GAE: 하나님이 약속하신 대로 이 사람의 후손에서 이스라엘을 위하여 구주를 세우셨으니 곧 예수라
v.KJV: Of this man’s seed hath God according to his promise raised unto Israel a Saviour, Jesus:
v.NASB: 'From the descendants of this man, according to promise, God has brought to Israel a Savior, Jesus,
v.NIV: “From this man’s descendants God has brought to Israel the Savior Jesus, as he promised.
v.NRSV: Of this man’s posterity God has brought to Israel a Savior, Jesus, as he promised;
v.SAENEW: 하나님은 약속하신 대로 다윗의 후손 가운데서 구주를 세워 이스라엘에게 보내셨으니, 그가 곧 예수입니다.
v.WLB: 하나님께서는 약속하신 대로 다윗의 자손 가운데 구세주를 이스라엘에게 보내 주셨는데 그 사람이 바로 예수입니다.

v.CNV: 在他来临之前，约翰早已向以色列全民宣讲悔改的洗礼。
v.ESV: Before his coming, John had proclaimed a baptism of repentance to all the people of Israel.
v.GAE: 그가 오시기에 앞서 요한이 먼저 회개의 세례를 이스라엘 모든 백성에게 전파하니라
v.KJV: When John had first preached before his coming the baptism of repentance to all the people of Israel.
v.NASB: after John had proclaimed before His coming a baptism of repentance to all the people of Israel.
v.NIV: Before the coming of Jesus, John preached repentance and baptism to all the people of Israel.
v.NRSV: before his coming John had already proclaimed a baptism of repentance to all the people of Israel.
v.SAENEW: 그가 오시기 전에, 요한이 먼저 회개의 세례를 모든 이스라엘 백성에게 선포하였습니다.
v.WLB: 예수께서 오시기 전에는 요한이 이스라엘 모든 백성들에게 회개의 세례를 선포했습니다.

v.CNV: 约翰快要跑完他的路程的时候，说：‘你们以为我是谁？我不是基督。他是在我以后来的，我就是给他解脚上的鞋带也不配。’
v.ESV: And as John was finishing his course, he said, ‘What do you suppose that I am? I am not he. No, but behold, after me one is coming, the sandals of whose feet I am not worthy to untie.’
v.GAE: 요한이 그 달려갈 길을 마칠 때에 말하되 너희가 나를 누구로 생각하느냐 나는 그리스도가 아니라 내 뒤에 오시는 이가 있으니 나는 그 발의 신발끈을 풀기도 감당하지 못하리라 하였으니
v.KJV: And as John fulfilled his course, he said, Whom think ye that I am? I am not he. But, behold, there cometh one after me, whose shoes of his feet I am not worthy to loose.
v.NASB: 'And while John was completing his course, he kept saying, 'What do you suppose that I am? I am not He. But behold, one is coming after me the sandals of whose feet I am not worthy to untie.'
v.NIV: As John was completing his work, he said: ‘Who do you suppose I am? I am not the one you are looking for. But there is one coming after me whose sandals I am not worthy to untie.’
v.NRSV: And as John was finishing his work, he said, ‘What do you suppose that I am? I am not he. No, but one is coming after me; I am not worthy to untie the thong of the sandals on his feet.’
v.SAENEW: 요한이 자기의 달려갈 길을 거의 다 갔을 때에 말하기를 '여러분은 나를 누구로 생각하십니까? 나는 그리스도가 아닙니다. 그는 내 뒤에 오실 터인데, 나는 그의 신발끈을 풀어드릴 자격도 없는 사람입니다' 하였습니다.
v.WLB: 그리고 요한은 자기 사역을 끝마칠 무렵 ‘너희는 나를 누구라고 생각하느냐? 나는 그리스도가 아니다. 그러나 그리스도는 내 뒤에 오실 것인데 나는 그분의 신발끈을 풀 자격도 없는 사람이다’라고 말했습니다.

v.CNV: “弟兄们，亚伯拉罕的子孙，和你们中间敬畏　神的人哪，这救恩之道是传给我们的。
v.ESV: “Brothers, sons of the family of Abraham, and those among you who fear God, to us has been sent the message of this salvation.
v.GAE: 형제들아 아브라함의 후손과 너희 중 하나님을 경외하는 사람들아 이 구원의 말씀을 우리에게 보내셨거늘
v.KJV: Men and brethren, children of the stock of Abraham, and whosoever among you feareth God, to you is the word of this salvation sent.
v.NASB: 'Brethren, sons of Abraham's family, and those among you who fear God, to us the message of this salvation has been sent.
v.NIV: “Fellow children of Abraham and you God-fearing Gentiles, it is to us that this message of salvation has been sent.
v.NRSV: “My brothers, you descendants of Abraham’s family, and others who fear God, to us the message of this salvation has been sent.
v.SAENEW: 아브라함의 자손인 동포 여러분, 그리고 여러분 가운데서 하나님을 두려워하는 사람들이여, 하나님께서 이 구원의 말씀을 우리에게 보내셨습니다.
v.WLB: 아브라함의 자손인 형제들이여! 그리고 하나님을 경외하는 이방 사람들이여! 하나님께서 이 구원의 말씀을 우리에게 보내 주셨습니다.

v.CNV: 住在耶路撒冷的人和他们的官长，因为不认识基督，也不明白每逢安息日所读的先知的话，就把他定了罪，正好应验了先知的话。
v.ESV: For those who live in Jerusalem and their rulers, because they did not recognize him nor understand the utterances of the prophets, which are read every Sabbath, fulfilled them by condemning him.
v.GAE: 예루살렘에 사는 자들과 그들 관리들이 예수와 및 안식일마다 외우는 바 선지자들의 말을 알지 못하므로 예수를 정죄하여 선지자들의 말을 응하게 하였도다
v.KJV: For they that dwell at Jerusalem, and their rulers, because they knew him not, nor yet the voices of the prophets which are read every sabbath day, they have fulfilled them in condemning him.
v.NASB: 'For those who live in Jerusalem, and their rulers, recognizing neither Him nor the utterances of the prophets which are read every Sabbath, fulfilled these by condemning Him.
v.NIV: The people of Jerusalem and their rulers did not recognize Jesus, yet in condemning him they fulfilled the words of the prophets that are read every Sabbath.
v.NRSV: Because the residents of Jerusalem and their leaders did not recognize him or understand the words of the prophets that are read every sabbath, they fulfilled those words by condemning him.
v.SAENEW: 그런데 예루살렘에 사는 사람들과 그들의 지도자들이 이 예수를 알지 못하고, 안식일마다 읽는 예언자들의 말도 깨닫지 못해서, 그를 정죄함으로써, 예언자들의 말을 그대로 이루었습니다.
v.WLB: 예루살렘에 사는 사람들과 그 지도자들은 예수를 알아보기는커녕 오히려 예수를 정죄해 안식일마다 읽는 예언자들의 말씀을 그대로 이루었습니다.

v.CNV: 他们虽然找不出该死的罪状，还是要求彼拉多杀害他。
v.ESV: And though they found in him no guilt worthy of death, they asked Pilate to have him executed.
v.GAE: 죽일 죄를 하나도 찾지 못하였으나 빌라도에게 죽여 달라 하였으니
v.KJV: And though they found no cause of death in him, yet desired they Pilate that he should be slain.
v.NASB: 'And though they found no ground for putting Him to death, they asked Pilate that He be executed.
v.NIV: Though they found no proper ground for a death sentence, they asked Pilate to have him executed.
v.NRSV: Even though they found no cause for a sentence of death, they asked Pilate to have him killed.
v.SAENEW: 그들은 예수를 죽일 만한 아무런 까닭도 찾지 못하였지만, 빌라도에게 강요하여 예수를 죽이게 하였습니다.
v.WLB: 그들은 사형을 선고할 근거를 전혀 찾지 못했음에도 예수를 죽여 달라고 빌라도에게 요구하고

v.CNV: 他们把所记载一切关于他的事作成了，就把他从木头上取下来，放在坟墓里。
v.ESV: And when they had carried out all that was written of him, they took him down from the tree and laid him in a tomb.
v.GAE: 성경에 그를 가리켜 기록한 말씀을 다 응하게 한 것이라 후에 나무에서 내려다가 무덤에 두었으나
v.KJV: And when they had fulfilled all that was written of him, they took him down from the tree, and laid him in a sepulchre.
v.NASB: 'When they had carried out all that was written concerning Him, they took Him down from the cross and laid Him in a tomb.
v.NIV: When they had carried out all that was written about him, they took him down from the cross and laid him in a tomb.
v.NRSV: When they had carried out everything that was written about him, they took him down from the tree and laid him in a tomb.
v.SAENEW: 이와 같이, 그를 가리켜 기록한 것을 다 행한 뒤에, 그들은 예수의 시체를 나무에서 내려다가, 무덤에 두었습니다.
v.WLB: 예수에 관해 성경에 기록된 모든 말씀대로 행한 뒤 예수를 나무에서 끌어내려 무덤에 묻었습니다.

v.CNV: 但　神却使他从死人中复活了。
v.ESV: But God raised him from the dead,
v.GAE: 하나님이 죽은 자 가운데서 그를 살리신지라
v.KJV: But God raised him from the dead:
v.NASB: 'But God raised Him from the dead;
v.NIV: But God raised him from the dead,
v.NRSV: But God raised him from the dead;
v.SAENEW: 그러나 하나님께서 예수를 죽은 사람 가운데서 살리셨습니다.
v.WLB: 그러나 하나님께서는 죽은 사람들 가운데서 예수를 살리셨습니다.

v.CNV: 有许多日子，他向那些跟他一同从加利利上耶路撒冷的人显现，现在这些人在民众面前作了他的见证人，
v.ESV: and for many days he appeared to those who had come up with him from Galilee to Jerusalem, who are now his witnesses to the people.
v.GAE: 갈릴리로부터 예루살렘에 함께 올라간 사람들에게 여러 날 보이셨으니 그들이 이제 백성 앞에서 그의 증인이라
v.KJV: And he was seen many days of them which came up with him from Galilee to Jerusalem, who are his witnesses unto the people.
v.NASB: and for many days He appeared to those who came up with Him from Galilee to Jerusalem, the very ones who are now His witnesses to the people.
v.NIV: and for many days he was seen by those who had traveled with him from Galilee to Jerusalem. They are now his witnesses to our people.
v.NRSV: and for many days he appeared to those who came up with him from Galilee to Jerusalem, and they are now his witnesses to the people.
v.SAENEW: 그래서 예수는 자기와 함께 갈릴리에서 예루살렘으로 올라간 사람들에게 여러 날 동안 나타나 보이셨습니다. 이 사람들은 [지금] 백성에게 예수의 증인입니다.
v.WLB: 그 후 여러 날 동안 예수는 갈릴리로부터 예루살렘까지 자기와 동행했던 사람들에게 나타나셨습니다. 그들이 바로 지금 백성들에게 예수를 증거하는 사람들입니다.

v.CNV: 我们报好信息给你们：　神给列祖的应许，
v.ESV: And we bring you the good news that what God promised to the fathers,
v.GAE: 우리도 조상들에게 주신 약속을 너희에게 전파하노니
v.KJV: And we declare unto you glad tidings, how that the promise which was made unto the fathers,
v.NASB: 'And we preach to you the good news of the promise made to the fathers,
v.NIV: “We tell you the good news: What God promised our ancestors
v.NRSV: And we bring you the good news that what God promised to our ancestors
v.SAENEW: 우리는 하나님께서 조상들에게 하신 그 약속을 여러분에게 기쁜 소식으로 전합니다.
v.WLB: 우리도 여러분에게 하나님께서 우리 조상들에게 약속하신 좋은 소식을 전하고자 합니다.

v.CNV: 借着耶稣的复活，向我们这些作子孙的应验了。就如诗篇第二篇所记的：‘你是我的儿子，我今日生了你。’
v.ESV: this he has fulfilled to us their children by raising Jesus, as also it is written in the second Psalm, “‘You are my Son, today I have begotten you.’
v.GAE: 곧 하나님이 예수를 일으키사 우리 자녀들에게 이 약속을 이루게 하셨다 함이라 시편 둘째 편에 기록한 바와 같이 너는 내 아들이라 오늘 너를 낳았다 하셨고
v.KJV: God hath fulfilled the same unto us their children, in that he hath raised up Jesus again; as it is also written in the second psalm, Thou art my Son, this day have I begotten thee.
v.NASB: that God has fulfilled this promise to our children in that He raised up Jesus, as it is also written in the second Psalm, 'YOU ARE MY SON; TODAY I HAVE BEGOTTEN YOU.'
v.NIV: he has fulfilled for us, their children, by raising up Jesus. As it is written in the second Psalm: “‘You are my son; today I have become your father.’
v.NRSV: he has fulfilled for us, their children, by raising Jesus; as also it is written in the second psalm, ‘You are my Son; today I have begotten you.’
v.SAENEW: 하나님께서 예수를 일으키셔서, [조상들의] 후손인 우리에게 그 약속을 이루어 주셨습니다. 시편 둘째 편에 기록한 바 '너는 내 아들이다. 오늘 내가 너를 낳았다' 한 것과 같습니다.
v.WLB: 하나님께서는 예수를 살리심으로써 그들의 자손인 우리에게 그 약속이 이뤄지게 하셨습니다. 이것은 시편 2편에 기록된 것과 같습니다. ‘너는 내 아들이다. 오늘 내가 너를 낳았다.’

v.CNV: 至于　神使他从死人中复活，不再归于朽坏，他曾这样说：‘我必把应许大卫的、神圣可靠的恩福赐给你们。’
v.ESV: And as for the fact that he raised him from the dead, no more to return to corruption, he has spoken in this way, “‘I will give you the holy and sure blessings of David.’
v.GAE: 또 하나님께서 죽은 자 가운데서 그를 일으키사 다시 썩음을 당하지 않게 하실 것을 가르쳐 이르시되 내가 다윗의 거룩하고 미쁜 은사를 너희에게 주리라 하셨으며
v.KJV: And as concerning that he raised him up from the dead, now no more to return to corruption, he said on this wise, I will give you the sure mercies of David.
v.NASB: 'As for the fact that He raised Him up from the dead, no longer to return to decay, He has spoken in this way: 'I WILL GIVE YOU THE HOLY and SURE blessings OF DAVID.'
v.NIV: God raised him from the dead so that he will never be subject to decay. As God has said, “‘I will give you the holy and sure blessings promised to David.’
v.NRSV: As to his raising him from the dead, no more to return to corruption, he has spoken in this way, ‘I will give you the holy promises made to David.’
v.SAENEW: 하나님께서 그를 죽은 사람들 가운데서 살리시고, 다시는 썩지 않게 하셨는데, 이렇게 미리 말씀하셨습니다. '다윗에게 약속한 거룩하고 확실한 복을, 내가 너희에게 주겠다.'
v.WLB: 하나님께서는 죽은 사람들 가운데서 예수를 다시 살리시고 썩지 않게 하셨는데 이것은 ‘내가 다윗에게 약속한 거룩하고 확실한 복을 너희에게 줄 것이다’라고 말씀하셨던 것입니다.

v.CNV: 所以他在另一篇说：‘你必不容你的圣者见朽坏。’
v.ESV: Therefore he says also in another psalm, “‘You will not let your Holy One see corruption.’
v.GAE: 또 다른 시편에 일렀으되 주의 거룩한 자로 썩음을 당하지 않게 하시리라 하셨느니라
v.KJV: Wherefore he saith also in another psalm, Thou shalt not suffer thine Holy One to see corruption.
v.NASB: 'Therefore He also says in another Psalm, 'YOU WILL NOT ALLOW YOUR HOLY ONE TO UNDERGO DECAY.'
v.NIV: So it is also stated elsewhere: “‘You will not let your holy one see decay.’
v.NRSV: Therefore he has also said in another psalm, ‘You will not let your Holy One experience corruption.’
v.SAENEW: 그러므로 다른 시편에서는 또 이렇게 말씀하셨습니다. '주님께서는 주님의 거룩한 분이 썩지 않게 하실 것이다.'
v.WLB: 또 다른 시편에도 기록돼 있습니다. ‘주께서는 주의 거룩하신 분을 썩게 내버려 두지 않으실 것입니다.’

v.CNV: “大卫在他自己的世代里，遵行了　神的计划，就睡了，归回他列祖那里，见了朽坏。
v.ESV: For David, after he had served the purpose of God in his own generation, fell asleep and was laid with his fathers and saw corruption,
v.GAE: 다윗은 당시에 하나님의 뜻을 따라 섬기다가 잠들어 그 조상들과 함께 묻혀 썩음을 당하였으되
v.KJV: For David, after he had served his own generation by the will of God, fell on sleep, and was laid unto his fathers, and saw corruption:
v.NASB: 'For David, after he had served the purpose of God in his own generation, fell asleep, and was laid among his fathers and underwent decay;
v.NIV: “Now when David had served God’s purpose in his own generation, he fell asleep; he was buried with his ancestors and his body decayed.
v.NRSV: For David, after he had served the purpose of God in his own generation, died, was laid beside his ancestors, and experienced corruption;
v.SAENEW: 다윗은 사는 동안, 하나님의 뜻을 받들어 섬기고, 잠들어서 조상들 곁에 묻혀 썩고 말았습니다.
v.WLB: 다윗은 한평생 하나님의 뜻을 잘 받들다 결국 잠들어 자기 조상들과 함께 묻혔고 그 시체는 썩고 말았습니다.

v.CNV: 唯独　神所复活的那一位，没有见过朽坏。
v.ESV: but he whom God raised up did not see corruption.
v.GAE: 하나님께서 살리신 이는 썩음을 당하지 아니하였나니
v.KJV: But he, whom God raised again, saw no corruption.
v.NASB: but He whom God raised did not undergo decay.
v.NIV: But the one whom God raised from the dead did not see decay.
v.NRSV: but he whom God raised up experienced no corruption.
v.SAENEW: 그러나 하나님께서 살리신 분은 썩지 않으셨습니다.
v.WLB: 그러나 하나님께서 죽은 사람들 가운데서 살리신 분은 썩지 않으셨습니다.

v.CNV: 所以弟兄们，你们当知道，赦罪之道是由这位耶稣传给你们的。在你们靠摩西律法不能称义的一切事上，
v.ESV: Let it be known to you therefore, brothers, that through this man forgiveness of sins is proclaimed to you,
v.GAE: 그러므로 형제들아 너희가 알 것은 이 사람을 힘입어 죄 사함을 너희에게 전하는 이것이며
v.KJV: Be it known unto you therefore, men and brethren, that through this man is preached unto you the forgiveness of sins:
v.NASB: 'Therefore let it be known to you, brethren, that through Him forgiveness of sins is proclaimed to you,
v.NIV: “Therefore, my friends, I want you to know that through Jesus the forgiveness of sins is proclaimed to you.
v.NRSV: Let it be known to you therefore, my brothers, that through this man forgiveness of sins is proclaimed to you;
v.SAENEW: 그러므로 동포 여러분, 바로 이 예수로 말미암아 여러분에게 죄 용서가 선포된다는 것을 알아야 합니다.
v.WLB: 그러므로 내 형제들이여, 예수를 통해 죄 용서함 받을 수 있다는 소식이 여러분에게 선포되고 있음을 알기 바랍니다.

v.CNV: 信靠他的人就得称义了。
v.ESV: and by him everyone who believes is freed from everything from which you could not be freed by the law of Moses.
v.GAE: 또 모세의 율법으로 너희가 의롭다 하심을 얻지 못하던 모든 일에도 이 사람을 힘입어 믿는 자마다 의롭다 하심을 얻는 이것이라
v.KJV: And by him all that believe are justified from all things, from which ye could not be justified by the law of Moses.
v.NASB: and through Him everyone who believes is freed from all things, from which you could not be freed through the Law of Moses.
v.NIV: Through him everyone who believes is set free from every sin, a justification you were not able to obtain under the law of Moses.
v.NRSV: by this Jesus everyone who believes is set free from all those sins from which you could not be freed by the law of Moses.
v.SAENEW: 여러분이 모세의 율법으로는 의롭게 될 수 없던 그 모든 일에서 풀려납니다. 믿는 사람은 누구나 다 예수 안에서 의롭게 됩니다.
v.WLB: 여러분이 모세의 율법으로는 의롭게 될 수 없었던 모든 것에서도 예수를 믿는 사람은 누구나 의롭다고 인정을 받게 되는 것입니다.

v.CNV: 你们要小心，免得先知书上所说的临到你们：
v.ESV: Beware, therefore, lest what is said in the Prophets should come about:
v.GAE: 그런즉 너희는 선지자들을 통하여 말씀하신 것이 너희에게 미칠까 삼가라
v.KJV: Beware therefore, lest that come upon you, which is spoken of in the prophets;
v.NASB: 'Therefore take heed, so that the thing spoken of in the Prophets may not come upon you:
v.NIV: Take care that what the prophets have said does not happen to you:
v.NRSV: Beware, therefore, that what the prophets said does not happen to you:
v.SAENEW: 그러므로 예언서에서 말한 일이 여러분에게 일어나지 않도록 조심하십시오. 이렇게 말하였습니다.
v.WLB: 그러므로 예언자들이 말한 것이 여러분에게 일어나지 않도록 조심하십시오.

v.CNV: ‘傲慢的人哪！你们要看、要惊奇、要灭亡，因为在你们的日子，我要作一件事，就算有人告诉你们，你们总是不信。’”
v.ESV: “‘Look, you scoffers, be astounded and perish; for I am doing a work in your days, a work that you will not believe, even if one tells it to you.’”
v.GAE: 일렀으되 보라 멸시하는 사람들아 너희는 놀라고 멸망하라 내가 너희 때를 당하여 한 일을 행할 것이니 사람이 너희에게 일러줄지라도 도무지 믿지 못할 일이라 하였느니라 하니라
v.KJV: Behold, ye despisers, and wonder, and perish: for I work a work in your days, a work which ye shall in no wise believe, though a man declare it unto you.
v.NASB: 'BEHOLD, YOU SCOFFERS, AND MARVEL, AND PERISH; FOR I AM ACCOMPLISHING A WORK IN YOUR DAYS, A WORK WHICH YOU WILL NEVER BELIEVE, THOUGH SOMEONE SHOULD DESCRIBE IT TO YOU.''
v.NIV: “‘Look, you scoffers, wonder and perish, for I am going to do something in your days that you would never believe, even if someone told you.’ ”
v.NRSV: ‘Look, you scoffers! Be amazed and perish, for in your days I am doing a work, a work that you will never believe, even if someone tells you.’”
v.SAENEW: '보아라, 너희 비웃는 자들아, 놀라고 망하여라. 내가 너희 시대에 한 가지 일을 할 터인데, 그 일을 누가 너희에게 말하여 줄지라도 너희는 도무지 믿지 않을 것이다.'"
v.WLB: ‘보라. 너희 비웃는 사람들아! 너희는 놀라고 망하라. 내가 너희 시대에 한 가지 일을 행하겠다. 누군가 그 일을 너희에게 전해 준다 해도 너희가 믿지 않을 것이다.’”

v.CNV: 保罗和巴拿巴出来的时候，众人请求他们下一个安息日再对他们讲这些话。
v.ESV: As they went out, the people begged that these things might be told them the next Sabbath.
v.GAE: 그들이 나갈새 사람들이 청하되 다음 안식일에도 이 말씀을 하라 하더라
v.KJV: And when the Jews were gone out of the synagogue, the Gentiles besought that these words might be preached to them the next sabbath.
v.NASB: As Paul and Barnabas were going out, the people kept begging that these things might be spoken to them the next Sabbath.
v.NIV: As Paul and Barnabas were leaving the synagogue, the people invited them to speak further about these things on the next Sabbath.
v.NRSV: As Paul and Barnabas were going out, the people urged them to speak about these things again the next sabbath.
v.SAENEW: 그들이 회당에서 나올 때에, 사람들은 다음 안식일에도 이러한 말씀을 해 달라고 청하였다.
v.WLB: 바울과 바나바가 회당에서 나오는데 사람들이 그들에게 다음 안식일에도 이 말씀을 좀 더 해 달라며 부탁했습니다.

v.CNV: 散会以后，许多犹太人和归信犹太教的虔诚人，跟从了保罗和巴拿巴。两人对他们谈话，勉励他们要恒久住在　神的恩典中。
v.ESV: And after the meeting of the synagogue broke up, many Jews and devout converts to Judaism followed Paul and Barnabas, who, as they spoke with them, urged them to continue in the grace of God.
v.GAE: 회당의 모임이 끝난 후에 유대인과 유대교에 입교한 경건한 사람들이 많이 바울과 바나바를 따르니 두 사도가 더불어 말하고 항상 하나님의 은혜 가운데 있으라 권하니라
v.KJV: Now when the congregation was broken up, many of the Jews and religious proselytes followed Paul and Barnabas: who, speaking to them, persuaded them to continue in the grace of God.
v.NASB: Now when the meeting of the synagogue had broken up, many of the Jews and of the God-fearing proselytes followed Paul and Barnabas, who, speaking to them, were urging them to continue in the grace of God.
v.NIV: When the congregation was dismissed, many of the Jews and devout converts to Judaism followed Paul and Barnabas, who talked with them and urged them to continue in the grace of God.
v.NRSV: When the meeting of the synagogue broke up, many Jews and devout converts to Judaism followed Paul and Barnabas, who spoke to them and urged them to continue in the grace of God.
v.SAENEW: 회중이 흩어진 뒤에도, 유대 사람들과 경건한 개종자들이 바울과 바나바를 많이 따랐다. 바울과 바나바는 그들에게 말을 걸면서, 늘 하나님의 은혜에 머물러 있으라고 권하였다.
v.WLB: 집회가 다 끝난 후 많은 유대 사람들과 유대교로 개종한 경건한 사람들이 바울과 바나바를 따라왔습니다. 그들은 그 사람들과 함께 이야기를 나누고 하나님의 은혜 가운데 계속 머물러 있으라고 권했습니다.

v.CNV: 下一个安息日，几乎全城的人都聚了来，要听主的道。
v.ESV: The next Sabbath almost the whole city gathered to hear the word of the Lord.
v.GAE: 그 다음 안식일에는 온 시민이 거의 다 하나님의 말씀을 듣고자 하여 모이니
v.KJV: And the next sabbath day came almost the whole city together to hear the word of God.
v.NASB: The next Sabbath nearly the whole city assembled to hear the word of the Lord.
v.NIV: On the next Sabbath almost the whole city gathered to hear the word of the Lord.
v.NRSV: The next sabbath almost the whole city gathered to hear the word of the Lord.
v.SAENEW: 그 다음 안식일에는 온 동네 사람이 거의 다 하나님의 말씀을 들으려고 모여들었다.
v.WLB: 그 다음 안식일에는 그 도시의 사람들이 거의 다 주의 말씀을 듣기 위해 모였습니다.

v.CNV: 犹太人看见这么多人，就满心嫉妒，反驳保罗所讲的，并且毁谤他们。
v.ESV: But when the Jews saw the crowds, they were filled with jealousy and began to contradict what was spoken by Paul, reviling him.
v.GAE: 유대인들이 그 무리를 보고 시기가 가득하여 바울이 말한 것을 반박하고 비방하거늘
v.KJV: But when the Jews saw the multitudes, they were filled with envy, and spake against those things which were spoken by Paul, contradicting and blaspheming.
v.NASB: But when the Jews saw the crowds, they were filled with jealousy and began contradicting the things spoken by Paul, and were blaspheming.
v.NIV: When the Jews saw the crowds, they were filled with jealousy. They began to contradict what Paul was saying and heaped abuse on him.
v.NRSV: But when the Jews saw the crowds, they were filled with jealousy; and blaspheming, they contradicted what was spoken by Paul.
v.SAENEW: 유대 사람들이 그 무리를 보고 시기심으로 가득 차서, 바울과 바나바가 한 말을 반박하고 비방하였다.
v.WLB: 그 무리를 본 유대 사람들은 시기심으로 가득 차 바울이 말하는 것을 반대하며 모욕했습니다.

v.CNV: 保罗和巴拿巴却放胆说：“　神的道，先讲给你们听，是应该的。但因为你们弃绝这道，断定自己不配得永生，所以我们现在就转向外族人去了。
v.ESV: And Paul and Barnabas spoke out boldly, saying, “It was necessary that the word of God be spoken first to you. Since you thrust it aside and judge yourselves unworthy of eternal life, behold, we are turning to the Gentiles.
v.GAE: 바울과 바나바가 담대히 말하여 이르되 하나님의 말씀을 마땅히 먼저 너희에게 전할 것이로되 너희가 그것을 버리고 영생을 얻기에 합당하지 않은 자로 자처하기로 우리가 이방인에게로 향하노라
v.KJV: Then Paul and Barnabas waxed bold, and said, It was necessary that the word of God should first have been spoken to you: but seeing ye put it from you, and judge yourselves unworthy of everlasting life, lo, we turn to the Gentiles.
v.NASB: Paul and Barnabas spoke out boldly and said, 'It was necessary that the word of God be spoken to you first; since you repudiate it and judge yourselves unworthy of eternal life, behold, we are turning to the Gentiles.
v.NIV: Then Paul and Barnabas answered them boldly: “We had to speak the word of God to you first. Since you reject it and do not consider yourselves worthy of eternal life, we now turn to the Gentiles.
v.NRSV: Then both Paul and Barnabas spoke out boldly, saying, “It was necessary that the word of God should be spoken first to you. Since you reject it and judge yourselves to be unworthy of eternal life, we are now turning to the Gentiles.
v.SAENEW: 그러나 바울과 바나바는 담대하게 말하였다. "우리는 하나님의 말씀을 당신들에게 먼저 전해야 하였습니다. 그러나 지금 당신들이 그것을 배척하고, 영원한 생명을 얻기에 합당하지 못한 사람으로 스스로 판정하므로, 우리는 이제 이방 사람들에게로 갑니다.
v.WLB: 그때 바울과 바나바가 그들에게 담대하게 대답했습니다. “우리는 하나님의 말씀을 여러분에게 먼저 전하지 않을 수 없었습니다. 그런데도 여러분은 그 말씀을 거절하고 영원한 생명을 얻기에 합당치 못한 사람이라고 스스로 판단하고 있습니다. 그렇기 때문에 우리는 이제 이방 사람들에게 눈을 돌립니다.

v.CNV: 因为主曾这样吩咐我们说：‘我已立你作外族人的光，使你把救恩带到地极去。’”
v.ESV: For so the Lord has commanded us, saying, “‘I have made you a light for the Gentiles, that you may bring salvation to the ends of the earth.’”
v.GAE: 주께서 이같이 우리에게 명하시되 내가 너를 이방의 빛으로 삼아 너로 땅 끝까지 구원하게 하리라 하셨느니라 하니
v.KJV: For so hath the Lord commanded us, saying, I have set thee to be a light of the Gentiles, that thou shouldest be for salvation unto the ends of the earth.
v.NASB: 'For so the Lord has commanded us, 'I HAVE PLACED YOU AS A LIGHT FOR THE GENTILES, THAT YOU MAY BRING SALVATION TO THE END OF THE EARTH.''
v.NIV: For this is what the Lord has commanded us: “‘I have made you a light for the Gentiles, that you may bring salvation to the ends of the earth.’ ”
v.NRSV: For so the Lord has commanded us, saying, ‘I have set you to be a light for the Gentiles, so that you may bring salvation to the ends of the earth.’”
v.SAENEW: 주님께서 우리에게 명하시기를 '내가 너를 뭇 민족의 빛으로 삼았으니, 그것은 네가 땅 끝까지 구원을 이루게 하려는 것이다' 하셨습니다."
v.WLB: 이것이 바로 주께서 우리에게 하신 명령이기 때문입니다. ‘내가 너를 이방 사람들의 빛으로 삼았으니 이는 네가 땅 끝까지 구원을 이루게 하려는 것이다.’”

v.CNV: 外族人听见了就欢喜，赞美主的道，凡指定得永生的都信了。
v.ESV: And when the Gentiles heard this, they began rejoicing and glorifying the word of the Lord, and as many as were appointed to eternal life believed.
v.GAE: 이방인들이 듣고 기뻐하여 하나님의 말씀을 찬송하며 영생을 주시기로 작정된 자는 다 믿더라
v.KJV: And when the Gentiles heard this, they were glad, and glorified the word of the Lord: and as many as were ordained to eternal life believed.
v.NASB: When the Gentiles heard this, they began rejoicing and glorifying the word of the Lord; and as many as had been appointed to eternal life believed.
v.NIV: When the Gentiles heard this, they were glad and honored the word of the Lord; and all who were appointed for eternal life believed.
v.NRSV: When the Gentiles heard this, they were glad and praised the word of the Lord; and as many as had been destined for eternal life became believers.
v.SAENEW: 이방 사람들은 이 말을 듣고 기뻐하며 주님의 말씀을 찬양하였고, 영원한 생명을 얻도록 정하신 사람은 모두 믿게 되었다.
v.WLB: 이 말을 들은 이방 사람들은 기뻐하며 주의 말씀을 찬양했습니다. 그리고 영원한 생명을 얻도록 선택된 사람들은 모두 믿게 됐습니다.

v.CNV: 于是主的道传遍那地。
v.ESV: And the word of the Lord was spreading throughout the whole region.
v.GAE: 주의 말씀이 그 지방에 두루 퍼지니라
v.KJV: And the word of the Lord was published throughout all the region.
v.NASB: And the word of the Lord was being spread through the whole region.
v.NIV: The word of the Lord spread through the whole region.
v.NRSV: Thus the word of the Lord spread throughout the region.
v.SAENEW: 이렇게 해서 주님의 말씀이 그 온 지방에 퍼져 나갔다.
v.WLB: 그리하여 주의 말씀이 그 지방 전체에 두루 퍼졌습니다.

v.CNV: 但犹太人唆使虔诚尊贵的妇女和城内的显要，煽动大家迫害保罗和巴拿巴，把他们驱逐出境。
v.ESV: But the Jews incited the devout women of high standing and the leading men of the city, stirred up persecution against Paul and Barnabas, and drove them out of their district.
v.GAE: 이에 유대인들이 경건한 귀부인들과 그 시내 유력자들을 선동하여 바울과 바나바를 박해하게 하여 그 지역에서 쫓아내니
v.KJV: But the Jews stirred up the devout and honourable women, and the chief men of the city, and raised persecution against Paul and Barnabas, and expelled them out of their coasts.
v.NASB: But the Jews incited the devout women of prominence and the leading men of the city, and instigated a persecution against Paul and Barnabas, and drove them out of their district.
v.NIV: But the Jewish leaders incited the God-fearing women of high standing and the leading men of the city. They stirred up persecution against Paul and Barnabas, and expelled them from their region.
v.NRSV: But the Jews incited the devout women of high standing and the leading men of the city, and stirred up persecution against Paul and Barnabas, and drove them out of their region.
v.SAENEW: 그러나 유대 사람들은 경건한 귀부인들과 그 성의 지도층 인사들을 선동해서, 바울과 바나바를 박해하게 하였고, 그들을 그 지방에서 내쫓았다.
v.WLB: 그러나 유대 사람들은 경건한 귀부인들과 그 도시의 지도자들을 선동해 바울과 바나바를 핍박하게 했고 결국 그 지방에서 그들을 강제로 내쫓았습니다.

v.CNV: 两人当众跺掉脚上的尘土，往以哥念去了。
v.ESV: But they shook off the dust from their feet against them and went to Iconium.
v.GAE: 두 사람이 그들을 향하여 발의 티끌을 떨어 버리고 이고니온으로 가거늘
v.KJV: But they shook off the dust of their feet against them, and came unto Iconium.
v.NASB: But they shook off the dust of their feet in protest against them and went to Iconium.
v.NIV: So they shook the dust off their feet as a warning to them and went to Iconium.
v.NRSV: So they shook the dust off their feet in protest against them, and went to Iconium.
v.SAENEW: 그래서 바울과 바나바는 그들에게 발의 먼지를 떨어버리고, 이고니온으로 갔다.
v.WLB: 그러자 바울과 바나바는 발에 붙어 있던 먼지를 그들을 향해 떨어 버리고 이고니온으로 갔습니다.

v.CNV: 门徒满有喜乐，又被圣灵充满。
v.ESV: And the disciples were filled with joy and with the Holy Spirit.
v.GAE: 제자들은 기쁨과 성령이 충만하니라
v.KJV: And the disciples were filled with joy, and with the Holy Ghost.
v.NASB: And the disciples were continually filled with joy and with the Holy Spirit.
v.NIV: And the disciples were filled with joy and with the Holy Spirit.
v.NRSV: And the disciples were filled with joy and with the Holy Spirit.
v.SAENEW: 제자들은 기쁨과 성령으로 가득 차 있었다.
v.WLB: 제자들은 기쁨과 성령으로 충만했습니다.
